manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: india, watch, vacuum, draft, himalayas, marble, pie, bug, helicopter.
Your opponent's team words are: fork, deck, china, organ, card, thief, mint, spike.
Distractor words are: server, wall, car, cycle, shakespeare, ice, duck.
The kill words are: buffalo.

message.content:
CLUE: air
TARGETS: helicopter, vacuum, draft
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts. Provide clue and list of target words. Should be short. Choose a clue that connects multiple of our words.

Our words: india, watch, vacuum, draft, himalayas, marble? Actually "marble". pie, bug, helicopter.

We can try "mountain" relates to himalayas, maybe also marble? Not really. "Swiss" relates to watch, marble? hmm.

Maybe "air" relates to helicopter, vacuum (air removal), draft (air draft). Also bug (air bug? maybe not). Could be "air". That's a clue: air relates to helicopter (air travel), vacuum (airless), draft (air draft), watch? maybe not. Could be "time" relates to watch, draft (draft time?), not.

"Space" maybe relates to vacuum, helicopter (space shuttle?), not.

Maybe "engine" relates to helicopter, bug (engine bug?), not.

"Chocolate" for pie, maybe not.

"Indian" relates to india, but cannot use part? clue "subcontinent"? maybe.

Better: clue "cold" could relate to Himalayas (cold), vacuum (cold vacuum?), watch (cold watch?), not.

Clue "draft" is already a word, can't.

Clue "paper" relating to draft, maybe bug (paper bug?), not.

Clue "circuit" no.

We could choose to target only a subset, e.g., "air" for helicopter, vacuum, draft. That's 3. That seems safe. Need to ensure clue not part of any words: "air" not in list. It's okay.

Check other words: doesn't overlap with opponent or distractor list? No.

Thus give:

CLUE: air
TARGETS: helicopter, vacuum, draft

Make sure format.

Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'air'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

buffalo, shakespeare, organ, himalayas, watch, india, marble, car, fork, card, vacuum, deck, thief, server, china, cycle, duck, bug, draft, spike, wall, mint, helicopter, ice, pie
